nodes.x: Hold not thy peace, O God of my praise;
Keep not thou silence, O God: hold not thy peace, and be not still, O God.
O Lord, open thou my lips; and my mouth shall shew forth thy praise.
Unto thee, O my strength, will I sing: for God is my defence, and the God of my mercy.
Praise ye the Lord. Praise the Lord, O my soul.
Let my mouth be filled with thy praise and with thy honour all the day.
For the mouth of the wicked and the mouth of the deceitful are opened against me: they have spoken against me with a lying tongue.
My lips shall not speak wickedness, nor my tongue utter deceit.
For my mouth shall speak truth; and wickedness is an abomination to my lips.
Woe unto them! for they have fled from me: destruction unto them! because they have transgressed against me: though I have redeemed them, yet they have spoken lies against me.
The lips of the righteous know what is acceptable: but the mouth of the wicked speaketh frowardness.
My tongue also shall talk of thy righteousness all the day long: for they are confounded, for they are brought unto shame, that seek my hurt.
They compassed me about also with words of hatred; and fought against me without a cause.
They compassed me about; yea, they compassed me about: but in the name of the Lord I will destroy them.
They came round about me daily like water; they compassed me about together.
And it came to pass that after they had bound me insomuch that I could not move, the compass, which had been prepared of the Lord, did cease to work.
They that hate me without a cause are more than the hairs of mine head: they that would destroy me, being mine enemies wrongfully, are mighty: then I restored that which I took not away.
They compassed me about like bees; they are quenched as the fire of thorns: for in the name of the Lord I will destroy them.
For my love they are my adversaries: but I give myself unto prayer.
But I say unto you, Love your enemies, bless them that curse you, do good to them that hate you, and pray for them which despitefully use you, and persecute you;
But behold I say unto you, love your enemies, bless them that curse you, do good to them that hate you, and pray for them who despitefully use you and persecute you;
I pray for them: I pray not for the world, but for them which thou hast given me; for they are thine.
Neither pray I for these alone, but for them also which shall believe on me through their word;

Be thou humble, and the Lord thy God shall lend thee by thy hand, and give thee answer to thy prayers. I know thy heart, and have heard thy prayers concerning thy brethren. Bo not partial towards them ia love above many others ; hut let thy love be for them as for thyself, and let tliy love abound unto all men, and unto all who love iny name ; and pray for thy brethren of the Twelvo ; admonish…
And they have rewarded me evil for good, and hatred for my love.
They rewarded me evil for good to the spoiling of my soul.
Every day they wrest my words: all their thoughts are against me for evil.
Let this be the reward of mine adversaries from the Lord, and of them that speak evil against my soul.
They also that render evil for good are mine adversaries; because I follow the thing that good is.
If it do evil in my sight, that it obey not my voice, then I will repent of the good, wherewith I said I would benefit them.
Set thou a wicked man over him: and let Satan stand at his right hand.
The hands of the witnesses shall be first upon him to put him to death, and afterward the hands of all the people. So thou shalt put the evil away from among you.
Then saith Jesus unto him, Get thee hence, Satan: for it is written, Thou shalt worship the Lord thy God, and him only shalt thou serve.

Then hear thou from heaven, and do, and judge thy servants, by requiting the wicked, by recompensing his way upon his own head; and by justifying the righteous, by giving him according to his righteousness.
Then shall he say also unto them on the left hand, Depart from me, ye cursed, into everlasting fire, prepared for the devil and his angels:
And the Lord said unto Satan, Behold, he is in thine hand; but save his life.
When he shall be judged, let him be condemned: and let his prayer become sin.
And when he is come, he will reprove the world of sin, and of righteousness, and of judgment:
He shall judge thy people with righteousness, and thy poor with judgment.
And he shall rise the third day from the dead; and behold, he standeth to judge the world; and behold, all these things are done that a righteous judgment might come upon the children of men.
For he will not lay upon man more than right; that he should enter into judgment with God.
And let him repent of his sins, for he seeketh the praise of the world.
Let his days be few; and let another take his office.
Turn from him, that he may rest, till he shall accomplish, as an hireling, his day.
Let him eschew evil, and do good; let him seek peace, and ensue it.
Let his children be fatherless, and his wife a widow.
For this cause shall a man leave his father and mother, and cleave to his wife;
Let there be none to extend mercy unto him: neither let there be any to favour his fatherless children.
If his master have given him a wife, and she have born him sons or daughters; the wife and her children shall be her master’s, and he shall go out by himself.
And if he have no daughter, then ye shall give his inheritance unto his brethren.
And if he have betrothed her unto his son, he shall deal with her after the manner of daughters.
Let his children be continually vagabonds, and beg: let them seek their bread also out of their desolate places.

Begging, if Saints will feed poor willingly, their children will not be
found, 336.
His children shall seek to please the poor, and his hands shall restore their goods.

and meat for money to carry them to the devil; now stop that. I say to you Bishops, appoint assistants to visit every house in your Wards, and instruct them to take the liberty of lifting up the chest lids, and of looking under the floors and under the beds, for I tell you that some will hide their provisions and lie to you, and tell you that they have nothing, while they are getting money for…
Let the extortioner catch all that he hath; and let the strangers spoil his labour.
Let him that stole steal no more: but rather let him labour, working with his hands the thing which is good, that he may have to give to him that needeth.
Lest strangers be filled with thy wealth; and thy labours be in the house of a stranger;
Deliver me from the workers of iniquity, and save me from bloody men.
Let mine outcasts dwell with thee, Moab; be thou a covert to them from the face of the spoiler: for the extortioner is at an end, the spoiler ceaseth, the oppressors are consumed out of the land.
¶ If a man shall deliver unto his neighbour money or stuff to keep, and it be stolen out of the man’s house; if the thief be found, let him pay double.
Let there be none to extend mercy unto him: neither let there be any to favour his fatherless children.

Therefore the Lord shall have no joy in their young men, neither shall have mercy on their fatherless and widows: for every one is an hypocrite and an evildoer, and every mouth speaketh folly. For all this his anger is not turned away, but his hand is stretched out still.
The fathers shall not be put to death for the children, neither shall the children be put to death for the fathers: every man shall be put to death for his own sin.

clare most emphatically that he is a God of mercy, and a God of love. Can we, even in our degraded condition; consider a being endowed in the least degree with the attributes of love and mercy, or even of justice, who would consign millions of his creatures to endless torment because they do not believe and obey a doctrine which they never heard? Why such an idea is unworthy of intelligent…
He that spared not his own Son, but delivered him up for us all, how shall he not with him also freely give us all things?
But the mercy of the Lord is from everlasting to everlasting upon them that fear him, and his righteousness unto children’s children;
Let his posterity be cut off; and in the generation following let their name be blotted out.
In his humiliation his judgment was taken away: and who shall declare his generation? for his life is taken from the earth.

If Gen. Hyrum Smith will allow his name to be brought forth we go it for him, and we know from the confidence and respect that is entertained of him as a gentleman and a patriot he will be elected. Wbatsay you General ?
But first must he suffer many things, and be rejected of this generation.
Break it off, O Lord; break it off from the necks of thy servants, by thy power, that we may rise up in the midst of this generation and do thy work.
Let the iniquity of his fathers be remembered with the Lord; and let not the sin of his mother be blotted out.
Our fathers have sinned, and are not; and we have borne their iniquities.
We have sinned with our fathers, we have committed iniquity, we have done wickedly.
I will be his father, and he shall be my son. If he commit iniquity, I will chasten him with the rod of men, and with the stripes of the children of men:
We acknowledge, O Lord, our wickedness, and the iniquity of our fathers: for we have sinned against thee.
¶ Now, lo, if he beget a son, that seeth all his father’s sins which he hath done, and considereth, and doeth not such like,
Let them be before the Lord continually, that he may cut off the memory of them from the earth.
Let them be blotted out of the book of the living, and not be written with the righteous.
They shall be carried to Babylon, and there shall they be until the day that I visit them, saith the Lord; then will I bring them up, and restore them to this place.
Watch ye, and keep them, until ye weigh them before the chief of the priests and the Levites, and chief of the fathers of Israel, at Jerusalem, in the chambers of the house of the Lord.
Consume them in wrath, consume them, that they may not be: and let them know that God ruleth in Jacob unto the ends of the earth. Selah.
Because that he remembered not to shew mercy, but persecuted the poor and needy man, that he might even slay the broken in heart.
To him which led his people through the wilderness: for his mercy endureth for ever.
And that he might make known the riches of his glory on the vessels of mercy, which he had afore prepared unto glory,
But God, who is rich in mercy, for his great love wherewith he loved us,
For then must he often have suffered since the foundation of the world: but now once in the end of the world hath he appeared to put away sin by the sacrifice of himself.
As he loved cursing, so let it come unto him: as he delighted not in blessing, so let it be far from him.

And he said: Thus saith the Lord God—Cursed shall be the land, yea, this land, unto every nation, kindred, tongue, and people, unto destruction, which do wickedly, when they are fully ripe; and as I have said so shall it be; for this is the cursing and the blessing of God upon the land, for the Lord cannot look upon sin with the least degree of allowance.
He that saith unto the wicked, Thou art righteous; him shall the people curse, nations shall abhor him:

Then said he unto me, This is the curse that goeth forth over the face of the whole earth: for every one that stealeth shall be cut off as on this side according to it; and every one that sweareth shall be cut off as on that side according to it.
But to them that rebuke him shall be delight, and a good blessing shall come upon them.
As he clothed himself with cursing like as with his garment, so let it come into his bowels like water, and like oil into his bones.
And he that toucheth the flesh of him that hath the issue shall wash his clothes, and bathe himself in water, and be unclean until the even.
And he that burneth them shall wash his clothes, and bathe his flesh in water, and afterward he shall come into the camp.
After that he poureth water into a basin, and began to wash the disciples’ feet, and to wipe them with the towel wherewith he was girded.
But put forth thine hand now, and touch his bone and his flesh, and he will curse thee to thy face.
And whomsoever he toucheth that hath the issue, and hath not rinsed his hands in water, he shall wash his clothes, and bathe himself in water, and be unclean until the even.
Let it be unto him as the garment which covereth him, and for a girdle wherewith he is girded continually.

He shall put on the holy linen coat, and he shall have the linen breeches upon his flesh, and shall be girded with a linen girdle, and with the linen mitre shall he be attired: these are holy garments; therefore shall he wash his flesh in water, and so put them on.
For that is his covering only, it is his raiment for his skin: wherein shall he sleep? and it shall come to pass, when he crieth unto me, that I will hear; for I am gracious.

And he answered and spake unto those that stood before him, saying, Take away the filthy garments from him. And unto him he said, Behold, I have caused thine iniquity to pass from thee, and I will clothe thee with change of raiment.

And I will clothe him with thy robe, and strengthen him with thy girdle, and I will commit thy government into his hand: and he shall be a father to the inhabitants of Jerusalem, and to the house of Judah.
Let this be the reward of mine adversaries from the Lord, and of them that speak evil against my soul.
He shall reward evil unto mine enemies: cut them off in thy truth.
And the Lord shall deliver me from every evil work, and will preserve me unto his heavenly kingdom: to whom be glory for ever and ever. Amen.
And they shall know that I am the Lord, and that I have not said in vain that I would do this evil unto them.

Therefore this is thy gift; apply unto it, and blessed art thou, for it shall deliver you out of the hands of your enemies, when, if it were not so, they would slay you and bring your soul to destruction.
But do thou for me, O God the Lord, for thy name’s sake: because thy mercy is good, deliver thou me.
Hear, O Lord, and have mercy upon me: Lord, be thou my helper.
Let thy mercy, O Lord, be upon us, according as we hope in thee.
I will freely sacrifice unto thee: I will praise thy name, O Lord; for it is good.
Look thou upon me, and be merciful unto me, as thou usest to do unto those that love thy name.
Not unto us, O Lord, not unto us, but unto thy name give glory, for thy mercy, and for thy truth’s sake.
For I am poor and needy, and my heart is wounded within me.
Bow down thine ear, O Lord, hear me: for I am poor and needy.
But I am poor and needy; yet the Lord thinketh upon me: thou art my help and my deliverer; make no tarrying, O my God.
¶ When I would comfort myself against sorrow, my heart is faint in me.
Open thy mouth, judge righteously, and plead the cause of the poor and needy.
I am gone like the shadow when it declineth: I am tossed up and down as the locust.
Else, if thou refuse to let my people go, behold, to morrow will I bring the locusts into thy coast:
My days are like a shadow that declineth; and I am withered like grass.
When I lie down, I say, When shall I arise, and the night be gone? and I am full of tossings to and fro unto the dawning of the day.
My knees are weak through fasting; and my flesh faileth of fatness.
¶ Strengthen ye the weak hands, and confirm the feeble knees.
Why did the knees prevent me? or why the breasts that I should suck?

“Strengthen the Feeble Knees” By Elder Marvin J. Ashton Of the Quorum of the Twelve Apostles There is a phrase used four times in the standard works which has always intrigued me. It is the expression “feeble knees.” By definition, feeble means weak, not strong, without force, easily broken, frail. When Frederick G. Williams was called to be a Counselor to Joseph Smith, he was given this charge:…
In weariness and painfulness, in watchings often, in hunger and thirst, in fastings often, in cold and nakedness.
I am poured out like water, and all my bones are out of joint: my heart is like wax; it is melted in the midst of my bowels.
I became also a reproach unto them: when they looked upon me they shaked their heads.
Mine enemies reproach me all the day; and they that are mad against me are sworn against me.
I was a reproach among all mine enemies, but especially among my neighbours, and a fear to mine acquaintance: they that did see me without fled from me.
Thus hath the Lord dealt with me in the days wherein he looked on me, to take away my reproach among men.
Turn away my reproach which I fear: for thy judgments are good.
They have gaped upon me with their mouth; they have smitten me upon the cheek reproachfully; they have gathered themselves together against me.
Help me, O Lord my God: O save me according to thy mercy:
Heal me, O Lord, and I shall be healed; save me, and I shall be saved: for thou art my praise.
Save me, O God, by thy name, and judge me by thy strength.
Shew us thy mercy, O Lord, and grant us thy salvation.
Have mercy upon me, O God, according to thy lovingkindness: according unto the multitude of thy tender mercies blot out my transgressions.
That they may know that this is thy hand; that thou, Lord, hast done it.
That they may see, and know, and consider, and understand together, that the hand of the Lord hath done this, and the Holy One of Israel hath created it.
Therefore, behold, I will this once cause them to know, I will cause them to know mine hand and my might; and they shall know that my name is The Lord.
Thy hands have made me and fashioned me: give me understanding, that I may learn thy commandments.
But when thou doest alms let not thy left hand know what thy right hand doeth;
But when thou doest alms, let not thy left hand know what thy right hand doeth:
Let them curse, but bless thou: when they arise, let them be ashamed; but let thy servant rejoice.
Give them sorrow of heart, thy curse unto them.
Also take no heed unto all words that are spoken; lest thou hear thy servant curse thee:
Let mine adversaries be clothed with shame, and let them cover themselves with their own confusion, as with a mantle.
Let them be ashamed and brought to confusion together that rejoice at mine hurt: let them be clothed with shame and dishonour that magnify themselves against me.
Let all mine enemies be ashamed and sore vexed: let them return and be ashamed suddenly.
Let them be confounded and consumed that are adversaries to my soul; let them be covered with reproach and dishonour that seek my hurt.
Thou hast known my reproach, and my shame, and my dishonour: mine adversaries are all before thee.
Let them be ashamed and confounded together that seek after my soul to destroy it; let them be driven backward and put to shame that wish me evil.
I will greatly praise the Lord with my mouth; yea, I will praise him among the multitude.
In God will I praise his word: in the Lord will I praise his word.
I will give thee thanks in the great congregation: I will praise thee among much people.
I will bless the Lord at all times: his praise shall continually be in my mouth.
Praise ye the Lord. I will praise the Lord with my whole heart, in the assembly of the upright, and in the congregation.
I will praise thee, O Lord, among the people: and I will sing praises unto thee among the nations.
For he shall stand at the right hand of the poor, to save him from those that condemn his soul.
He shall spare the poor and needy, and shall save the souls of the needy.
He shall judge the poor of the people, he shall save the children of the needy, and shall break in pieces the oppressor.
He shall redeem their soul from deceit and violence: and precious shall their blood be in his sight.

But with righteousness shall he judge the poor, and reprove with equity for the meek of the earth; and he shall smite the earth with the rod of his mouth, and with the breath of his lips shall he slay the wicked.

But with righteousness shall he judge the poor, and reprove with equity for the meek of the earth: and he shall smite the earth with the rod of his mouth, and with the breath of his lips shall he slay the wicked.
